top_signals.title: Shipping firms shun Hormuz as U.S.–Iran clashes escalate, risk surges
top_signals.summary: As armed clashes between the United States and Iran continue, scuttling end-of-war talks and prompting the United States to reimpose a counter-blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, global shipping companies are reportedly forgoing transits because they do not trust the U.S. plan to escort ships through the strait. On the 15th, Reuters, citing multiple industry ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: IEA Predicts First Decrease in Global Oil Demand Since 2020
top_signals.summary: Global oil demand could decrease for the first time since 2020, following months of oil trade restrictions in the Middle East and ongoing geopolitical turmoil, the International Energy Agency (IEA) said on 10th July. This expectation is echoed in OPEC's most recent 2026 global oil demand growth forecast. The global demand for oil is expected to fall by around 1 million bpd year-on-year in 2026, which would be the first annual decline since that which was experienced during the Covid-19 pandemic in 2020, according to a recent IEA report. The IEA…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Tanker Market: Hormuz Strikes Cause “Deja-Vu”
top_signals.summary: The tanker markets have returned to their “wartime pattern” of behavior, after the latest round of strikes in the Middle East. top_signals.title: Panamax congestion in China: Two forces converge at berth
top_signals.summary: The queue of laden Panamaxes waiting to berth in China has elongated in recent weeks, driven by two overlapping forces. Inland, a wave of mine safety suspensions in Shanxi triggered a surge in seaborne coal imports that southern terminals are unable to absorb. Offshore, successive typhoons have slowed discharges. top_signals.title: Tanker owners weigh USGC as Hormuz tensions compromise Persian Gulf flows
top_signals.summary: Tanker owners are eyeing the US Gulf Coast as renewed tensions at the Strait of Hormuz threaten to disrupt Persian Gulf crude flows, though tepid VLCC activity out of the region and stronger returns on Brazil-China routes are tempering any immediate surge in US export freight rates. top_signals.title: Baltic Dry Index Falls for 2nd Day
top_signals.summary: The Baltic Exchange’s dry bulk freight index, which monitors rates for ships carrying dry bulk commodities, was down for the second session on Thursday, dropping about 3% to its lowest since July 6 at 2,840 points, amid continued weakness in the larger segment. The capesize index, which typically transports 150,000-ton cargoes including iron ore and ...

alerts.title: SEA-LNG 2026 Mid-Year Market Review underlines a decade of progress along methane decarbonisation pathway
alerts.summary: SEA-LNG, the multi-sector coalition spanning the entire LNG value chain, has published its 2026 Mid-Year Market Review. It provides a snapshot of the current market conditions facing the methane pathway, with particular focus on the growth of liquefied biomethane (LBM/bio-LNG). This report comes as SEA-LNG celebrates its tenth anniversary and gains provisional consultative status at ...

alerts.title: Commission registers two European Citizens' Initiatives on education and housing
alerts.summary: European Commission Press release Brussels, 17 Jul 2026 Today, the European Commission registered two European Citizens' Initiatives (ECIs) entitled: ‘All On Board - For your right to citizenship without borders' and ‘Right to Housing! Now and Forever'.
